message:
test(search): pin search_hit_key_doc_id byte-identical contract (num7z)

The perf rewrite in c861f5ef replaced the `format!` + repeated
`to_string` pattern with a pre-sized String + `write!` branches. The
stable dedup key is contractual: any drift silently breaks dedup
across fused hybrid search results.

Adds `search_hit_key_doc_id_matches_reference_byte_for_byte` which
keeps the prior `format!`-based implementation under
`search_hit_key_doc_id_reference_v0` inside the test module and
diff-checks the optimized output against it across five fixtures:
- populated i64/usize fields
- all-None optionals + empty strings
- extreme i64/usize/u64 boundary values + unicode title
- all-zero fields
- fields that themselves contain the 0x1F separator

Plus a structural probe that asserts exactly six 0x1F separators on
a fixture with no embedded separators, so accidental sep drops fail
loudly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
